mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate persistently above 8% signals significant slack in the labor market, suppressing wage growth and consumer spending. This negatively impacts forward-looking corporate earnings expectations and leads to lower equity valuations over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:US economy is in a slow recovery post-2008 financial crisis
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE is above 8.0% during a recovery period.

mechanism_narrative: In a Zero Interest Rate Policy (ZIRP) environment, accelerating inflation is a primary driver of fears that the central bank will be forced to tighten policy sooner than anticipated. This increases the equity risk premium and causes negative market returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS has been near zero since 2008
failure_condition: The correlation between the 3-month change in CPIAUCSL and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: The momentum of retail sales growth is a direct, high-frequency indicator of consumer health. A positive 3-month change in retail sales leads to upward revisions in near-term corporate earnings estimates, boosting equity prices.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 3-month change in RSAFS and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is consistently below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous deceleration in industrial production growth and acceleration in inflation signals a stagflationary environment. This combination compresses corporate profit margins via lower revenue growth and higher input costs, leading to negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year INDPRO growth < 2% AND Year-over-year CPIAUCSL growth > 3%
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the stagflationary trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp negative surprise in monthly nonfarm payrolls growth, specifically a net change below 50,000 jobs, signals a potential stall in the economic recovery. This triggers immediate risk-off sentiment and negative equity returns over a short horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:August 2011 jobs report showed zero net jobs added
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following a PAYEMS monthly change report below 50k is non-negative over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: In a fragile economic recovery following a housing market collapse, a material month-over-month increase in housing starts indicates a bottoming process and renewed construction activity. This boosts investor confidence in future GDP growth and supports equity prices.
evidence_refs: fact:US economy is recovering from the 2008 housing crisis
failure_condition: The signal's hit rate for predicting positive 20-day returns is below 55% during the 2010-2015 period when the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: The 6-month change in industrial production is a core indicator of economic momentum. Positive and accelerating production growth signals expanding economic activity, which translates to higher aggregate corporate revenues and positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 6-month change in INDPRO and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is less than 0.05 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in real GDP is the definition of a recession and signals a severe drop in aggregate demand. A quarter-over-quarter annualized decline in GDPC1 causes a major downward revision to earnings forecasts, leading to negative returns.
failure_condition: The average 60-day S&P 500 return is positive in quarters immediately following the release of a negative GDPC1 growth figure.
